title: Add calendar activation QA and apply step
description: Add a separate automation step for calendar activation after owner assignment, with availability and booking-rule QA.
acceptance_criteria: Activation refuses to run until owner assignment passes, availability/schedule is verified, booking notice/duration/interval/buffers are checked, and a free-slot smoke test passes; apply sends only the fields needed for activation.
notes: 2026-06-10: Completed calendar availability QA. Added reusable read-only calendar-availability-qa command using GET /calendars/{calendarId}/free-slots (Version 2021-04-15), default 14-day window, compact slot summaries, schedule field checks, and warnings. Updated activate-calendars --apply so verification includes the same free-slot smoke. Idempotent live verification wrote dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/calendar-activation-apply-with-availability-2026-06-10.json: both calendars skipped as already active, remained assigned to user 7f3lxWXzcFRp3QMYqotT, and availability passed. Consulta Gratuita returned 180 slots; On Site Visit returned 162 slots. openHoursCount=0 is a warning only because free-slots returned slots. Consulta Gratuita allows same-day booking (allowBookingAfter=0), flagged as client-confirm warning, not blocker.

title: Prove or automate GHL website publication
description: Find a safe way to publish generated HTML/images into the correct GHL Website Home block, or document the browser/manual flow with proof selectors.
acceptance_criteria: QC proves generated HTML is present in the correct GHL page block and public URL, not just account_websites or location.website; landing_form remains gated until Twilio approval.
notes: 2026-06-10: Closed after focused QA. Current HighLevel docs via Context7 expose GET /funnels/page, GET /funnels/page/count, and redirect endpoints, but no documented page-builder content/custom-HTML write or publish endpoint. Refreshed public preview QA at dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/ghl-profile9-preview-qa-2026-06-10.json: preview returned 200, hero/color markers present, Custom HTML placeholder absent, raw landing_form absent as expected because Twilio approval is pending. Confirmed WSL Profile 9 CDP reachable on http://127.0.0.1:9229 with authenticated Liverpool Digital GHL session; browser was on Contacts, so WSL map is profile reachability proof only. Preserved 2026-06-09 builder-block proof as acceptance evidence: generated HTML was saved to Home custom-code element #custom-code-MTo38o_zdB, reload hash matched 94e6e0a2830dafaf69a87d76c5a3375fa1ce6f89dd8949527e155cfbb0be69cd, and top-level Publish was not clicked.

title: Productize Supabase and PatronPro panel onboarding state access
description: Add documented, secret-safe access for the PatronPro panel/Supabase onboarding state so the automation can read generated HTML, images, form submissions, contact IDs, status flags, and approval gates without copy/paste.
acceptance_criteria: QC can read the Liverpool Digital generated HTML/images and onboarding status from Supabase or panel APIs using env-based credentials; no secrets are written to artifacts; missing access fails as blocked with exact env names and remediation.
notes: 2026-06-11: Supabase access is now unblocked and verified read-only. 1Password item 'Supabase - PATRON PRO' contains populated PUBLIC KEY, SECRET KEY, and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL (URL label currently has leading whitespace, so loaders should trim labels or use URL regex). Ran read-only export-docs and qc with Supabase + GHL credentials loaded in-process only. Artifacts: dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/export-docs-agent-supabase-2026-06-11.json, doc-pages/doc-pages.json, doc-pages/doc-pages.md, and qc-agent-supabase-ghl-2026-06-11.json. export-docs passed with pageCount=2. QC env passed for Supabase and GHL; statusCounts pass=12 fail=11 blocked=0. No DB writes were performed. Guardrail remains: no Supabase insert/update/delete/upsert/RPC/storage writes unless user explicitly requests them.

title: Confirm Brand Board create and update automation
description: Turn the current Brand Boards read finding into a safe automation step for applying client colors, with dry-run fixtures and explicit user confirmation before live Brand Board mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Automation can read existing Brand Boards, plan create/update payloads from generated or onboarding colors, apply only with explicit --apply, and QA proves the resulting main/accent/complementary colors match the selected palette.
notes: 2026-06-09: Brand Board create/update API proof completed for Liverpool Digital. Created Brand Board via POST /brand-boards/ with locationId, name, colors, default:true; then patched existing board via PATCH /brand-boards/{locationId}/{brandBoardId} to enforce default:true. Verification requires detail hydration because GET /brand-boards/{locationId} list can omit colors; detailed GET /brand-boards/{locationId}/{brandBoardId} verified default=true and colors #471F23, #F69309, #2F1417. Artifacts: brand-board-apply-2026-06-09.json (initial list-only verification gotcha), brand-board-default-apply-2026-06-09.json (final pass).

title: Resolve GHL contact form language and DND customization
description: Find a reliable automation path for the GHL Add Contact customize-form requirements: make Language required, remove DND Channels, and confirm the related setting that must be enabled in the same panel area.
acceptance_criteria: Either an official API/browser automation flow is implemented with QA screenshots/selectors, or the step is documented as manual with exact panel path, expected before/after state, and QC evidence requirements.
notes: 2026-06-10: quality-loop pass completed. API-first probe found no supported public GHL endpoint for Add Contact modal layout; layout guesses returned contact-id-style 400s. Live WSL Profile 9 browser fallback succeeded on https://app.gohighlevel.com/v2/location/4cPIvLND9hFAIzWQ1ZbL/settings/objects/6a26effcef5e1353b56f0e22/customize-record-modal. Selectors: [id="checkbox-contact.language"], #checkbox-dnd, #manage-fields-save-btn, [id="required-contact.language"], #save-button. QA: reopened Contacts -> Add Contact and verified Language * is present and DND/DnD is absent. Proof: dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/contact-form-profile9-add-contact-final-qa-2026-06-10.png and contact-form-profile9-add-contact-final-qa-map-2026-06-10.json.

title: Assign Liverpool Digital calendar owner via GHL API
description: Apply the approved safe live mutation that assigns the single Liverpool Digital user as the responsible team member for the two onboarding calendars.
acceptance_criteria: Both target calendar IDs from booking custom values show exactly one teamMembers userId matching the single location user; calendar activation remains unchanged and inactive.
notes: Live GHL apply succeeded for location 4cPIvLND9hFAIzWQ1ZbL. Consulta Gratuita (a2rL0hFhXf2sIdKZecoM) and On Site Visit (tWEirh80FGFIuCAIXGWb) each verify teamMembers[].userId = 7f3lxWXzcFRp3QMYqotT. Both calendars remain inactive by design; activation is tracked separately in ppweb-1.

title: Liverpool Digital PatronPro GHL documentation automation
description: Create a working, scriptable system for location 4cPIvLND9hFAIzWQ1ZbL / Liverpool Digital by ingesting PatronPro panel documentation, extracting required setup changes, implementing dry-run-first automation, and adding a QC verifier. No live PatronPro panel, Supabase, or GHL mutation is allowed until the required-change list is documented and the user explicitly approves the run mode.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation is captured in RLM; required panel/GHL changes are listed before mutation; automation scripts support dry-run mode; QC verifies every documented setup step; FSN1-compatible run notes exist; final report is stored under dev/agents/artifacts and doc/reports; branch is committed and pushed.
notes: Epic completed for dry-run automation/QC system. Live writes remain intentionally out of scope until explicit approval and credentials are provided.

title: Ingest PatronPro panel documentation into RLM
description: Export or inspect available PatronPro panel documentation from repo and/or panel APIs, normalize it into local artifacts, then store the useful documentation index and summaries in RLM.
acceptance_criteria: RLM entries include currentness metadata, source paths, artifact role, and Liverpool Digital relevance; local documentation artifact records exact sources and commands used.
notes: Source-code-derived panel/GHL documentation index stored locally and in RLM. Live doc_pages export remains gated by missing Supabase env or authenticated panel session; export-docs command is implemented for that condition.

title: User Supabase access check for PatronPro panel state
description: User/operator verifies how Supabase API access should be provided for PatronPro panel state so ppweb-3 can be unblocked without exposing secrets. Current state: local env lacks NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY; 1Password item 'Supabase - PATRON PRO' only exposes login-style fields, not API env fields.
acceptance_criteria: A secret-safe path exists for Codex to read panel state: either NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY are available via external env/structured 1Password item, or an authenticated panel API/session route is provided. Then rerun export-docs and qc, verify account_submissions/account_checklist/account_websites/contact IDs/approved_at/doc_pages are readable, and confirm no secrets appear in artifacts/git/RLM.
notes: 2026-06-11: User added Supabase URL/key fields to 1Password item. Verified one-pass read-only access from 'Supabase - PATRON PRO' in vault Picturelle: URL host mtkbqnngqcqywsdewaxs.supabase.co, PUBLIC KEY present, SECRET KEY present. Read-only REST smoke test against doc_pages returned HTTP 200 and rowCount=1. Then harness export-docs passed with pageCount=2 and QC passed Supabase/GHL env checks. No DB mutation was performed. Future use remains read-only unless user explicitly requests DB writes.

title: Guided manual QA walkthrough for Liverpool Digital onboarding
description: Oscar and Codex perform a guided manual review of every onboarding manual section for Liverpool Digital, comparing the manual requirements against GHL/panel evidence and the automation artifacts. Codex guides the operator step by step; operator verifies UI-only/client-gated items manually.
acceptance_criteria: Manual QA checklist is completed section by section: Fase 1 panel/form state, Fase 2 snapshot/contact form/calendars/domain/website/branding/language/notifications/workflows, Fase 3 Twilio/domain/Stripe/staff/calendar hours/app, and Fase 4 Twilio approval/form insertion/account activation. Each item is marked pass/fail/blocked/manual with evidence, and any mismatch becomes a new bead or an update to ppweb-elk.7/ppweb-3.
notes: 2026-06-11: Agent-side Supabase/GHL QA snapshot completed read-only. Artifacts: dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/qc-agent-supabase-ghl-2026-06-11.json, export-docs-agent-supabase-2026-06-11.json, doc-pages/doc-pages.json, doc-pages/doc-pages.md, and manual-qa-agent-walkthrough-2026-06-11.md. Agent QC counts: pass=12 fail=11 blocked=0. Manual QA remains open for Oscar to verify the same checklist in UI/process. No Supabase DB writes, GHL writes, publish, or account activation were performed.

title: Extract Liverpool Digital required setup checklist
description: Translate the Patreon Pro/PatronPro panel documentation into a concrete list of setup changes for location 4cPIvLND9hFAIzWQ1ZbL before any live mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Checklist names every required field, custom value, calendar/domain/phone/email/landing/Stripe/client access step, whether it is API-scriptable, UI-only, or needs AI reasoning, and its QC proof.
notes: Required pre-change list created at dev/agents/artifacts/doc/plan/liverpool-digital-required-change-list-2026-06-09.md and stored in RLM.

title: Build dry-run automation scripts
description: Create scripts that can read and prepare GHL/panel updates for the documented steps, defaulting to dry-run and requiring explicit run-mode selection for writes.
acceptance_criteria: Scripts accept the Liverpool Digital location ID, use env-based credentials only, print planned operations without secrets, and fail closed when credentials or approval flags are missing.
notes: Dry-run automation harness implemented with qc, plan, and export-docs modes. No live mutation commands are implemented.

title: Build QC verifier for documented setup steps
description: Create a read-only QC harness that checks each documentation-derived setup step after automation or manual work.
acceptance_criteria: QC output maps every check to pass/fail/blocked, source evidence, endpoint or panel source, timestamp, and remediation note.
notes: QC verifier implemented and dry-run output generated under dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/.

title: Prepare FSN1-compatible automation runbook
description: Document how the scripts can be run from FSN1 or tied into GHL automation without mixing this work into the separate FSN1 Slackbot branch.
acceptance_criteria: Runbook includes safe env paths, command examples, dry-run/write mode separation, logging destination, and rollback notes.
notes: FSN1 runbook and env template completed; runbook stored in RLM.

title: Produce final quality-loop report and handoff
description: Document each step performed, run the quality loop over the plan/artifacts, and produce a final report for Liverpool Digital automation.
acceptance_criteria: Report is saved under dev/agents/artifacts/doc/plan and copied to doc/reports/2026/06, and the final handoff includes bead IDs, verification, commit hash, push status, and RLM checkpoint.
notes: Quality-loop reviewer outputs, merge report, and finalized report completed and stored locally/RLM.

title: Browser automation fallback for GHL UI-only onboarding steps
description: After API-proven steps are exhausted, use a signed-in WSL browser profile with Playwright/Puppeteer to automate or QA GHL UI-only onboarding tasks such as Website editor publication, Contact Add form customization, DND Channels removal, platform language, and notification template settings.
acceptance_criteria: Run only after API attempts are documented; use an authenticated WSL browser profile; capture screenshots/selectors and before/after proof; do not bypass Twilio/Stripe/client gates; store successful patterns and failed gotchas in RLM.
notes: 2026-06-10 update: Browser fallback lane is now WSL Chrome Profile 9 on CDP http://127.0.0.1:9229 for PatronPro/GHL work; older Windows Profile 9 notes are historical evidence from the first website copy. Website editor proof for ppweb-2 is closed: generated HTML was saved into #custom-code-MTo38o_zdB and preview QA passes; Publish remains manual/operator-approved. Contact Add form Language/DND customization for ppweb-5 is also closed with WSL Profile 9 proof. Remaining browser-fallback targets: platform user language if API is unavailable, invoice/document notification template settings if API is unavailable, final publish/domain UI checks after phone/email/domain/Twilio gates, and any future GHL UI-only checklist items after API probes are documented.

title: Optimize generated website images for responsive AVIF delivery
description: Update the generated website workflow so AI-created website imagery is converted into responsive AVIF, WebP, and compressed JPEG variants, then published through GHL custom values in AVIF -> WebP -> JPEG order.
acceptance_criteria: Generated image route creates 640/960/1440 AVIF/WebP/JPEG variants per hero/about/contact image; GHL sync writes responsive srcset custom values plus JPEG fallback; website generation prompt uses picture markup with hero eager/fetchpriority and below-fold lazy loading; Lighthouse baseline report is stored in dev/agents artifacts.
notes: Implemented responsive image workflow: src/lib/website/image-variants.ts creates 640/960/1440 AVIF/WebP/JPEG variants via sharp; generate-images uploads variants to Supabase/GHL Media and writes srcset/fallback custom values; generate prompt now instructs HTML to use picture markup AVIF -> WebP -> JPEG with hero eager/fetchpriority and below-fold lazy images; Liverpool automation allowlist includes the new custom value keys. Baseline Lighthouse report stored at dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/lighthouse-image-performance-summary-2026-06-09.md/json and copied to /mnt/m/picturelle/devio/onboarding/doc/test-website. Verification: bunx tsc --noEmit passed; focused ESLint on touched files passed; repo-wide bun run lint still fails on unrelated existing lint errors in legal/onboarding/panel/support files. Caveat: current live Liverpool GHL page still uses previously published PNG HTML until generation and website publication are rerun.

title: Automate website SEO social preview and Lighthouse QA
description: Make generated PatronPro landing pages include repeatable SEO, Open Graph/WhatsApp/Messenger preview, structured data, and automated Lighthouse analysis as part of the onboarding website automation template.
acceptance_criteria: Generated HTML prompt requires canonical/meta description, Open Graph/Twitter preview tags using a 1200x630 website_social_image custom value, JSON-LD LocalBusiness/Service data, service/section anchors, and no JS-based GHL custom value reads; image generation creates/syncs website_social_image; a reusable Lighthouse JSON analyzer script outputs mobile/desktop scores, LCP, payload, image opportunities, and gotchas.
